crossEventQuestion: Should Microsoft authorize a large share repurchase program to signal commitment to shareholders?
observation: Microsoft authorized a $60 billion share repurchase program in 2024, reflecting a continued commitment to shareholders. In contrast, the 2013 authorization was for $40 billion, also reflecting a continued commitment to shareholders.
invariant: When the company has a commitment to returning cash to shareholders, authorizing a large share repurchase program is the chosen action.
boundaryConditions: The action must reflect a continued commitment to shareholders.
The board of directors must approve the program.

decisionAxis.decisionQuestion: How large should the share repurchase authorization be?
gatingVariables: Commitment to returning cash to shareholders

conditions: The company has a commitment to returning cash to shareholders.
The new share repurchase program is authorized up to $60 billion.
The authorization limit is set at $40 billion.
The board of directors must approve the program.

conditionDelta: The authorization limit increased from $40 billion (2013) to $60 billion (2024).
actionDelta: Proceed with the full $60 billion share repurchase authorization (2024) vs. Authorize the new share repurchase program up to $40 billion (2013)
